{{short description|Men's national association football team}}
{{About|the men's team|the women's team|Rwanda women's national football team}}
{{Use dmy dates|date=June 2021}}
{{Infobox national football team
| Name               = Rwanda
| Nickname           = ''Amavubi'' (The Wasps)
| Badge              = 
| Association        = [[Rwanda Football Federation|Fédération Rwandaise de Football Association]] (FERWAFA)
| Sub-confederation  = [[Council for East and Central Africa Football Associations|CECAFA]] (East Africa)
| Confederation      = [[Confederation of African Football|CAF]] (Africa)
| Coach              = [[Stephen Constantine]] 
| Captain            = [[Djihad Bizimana]]
| Most caps          = [[Haruna Niyonzima]] (112)
| Top scorer         = [[Olivier Karekezi]] (24)
| Home Stadium       = [[Amahoro Stadium]]
| FIFA Trigramme     = RWA
| FIFA Rank          = {{FIFA World Rankings|RWA}}
| FIFA max           = 64
| FIFA max date      = March 2015
| FIFA min           = 178
| FIFA min date      = July 1999
| Elo Rank           = {{World Football Elo Ratings|Rwanda}}
| Elo max            = 95
| Elo max date       = October 2008
| Elo min            = 150
| Elo min date       = July 1996
| pattern_la1        = 
| pattern_b1         =_RRNA25h
| pattern_ra1        =
| pattern_sh1        =_RRNA25h
| pattern_so1        =_RRNA25h
| leftarm1           = FCBE0A
| body1              = FCBE0A
| rightarm1          = FCBE0A
| shorts1            = 2E8A26
| socks1             = 0E260E
| pattern_la2        =
| pattern_b2         =_RRNA25a
| pattern_ra2        =
| pattern_sh2        =_RRNA25a
| pattern_so2        =_RRNA25a
| leftarm2           = 0A5AAE
| body2              = 0A5AAE
| rightarm2          = 0A5AAE
| shorts2            = FEFEFE
| socks2             = 0A5AAE

| First game         = {{fb|BDI|1967}} 6–2 {{fb-rt|RWA|1962}}<br>([[Libreville]], [[Gabon]]; 29 June 1976)
| Largest win        = {{nowrap begin}}{{fb|Rwanda}} 9–0 {{Fb-rt|DJI}} <br> ([[Dar es Salaam]], [[Tanzania]]; 13 December 2007){{nowrap end}}
| Largest loss       = {{fb|CMR}} 5–0 {{fb-rt|RWA|1962}}<br>([[Libreville]], [[Gabon]]; 7 July 1976)<br>{{fb|ZAI}} 6–1 {{fb-rt|RWA|1962}}<br>([[Gabon]]; 12 July 1976)<br>{{fb|TUN|1959}} 5–0 {{fb-rt|RWA|1962}}<br>([[Tunis]], [[Tunisia]]; 10 April 1983)<br>{{fb|UGA}} 5–0 {{fb-rt|RWA|1962}}<br>([[Kampala]], [[Uganda]]; 1 August 1998)

| Regional name      = [[Africa Cup of Nations]]
| Regional cup apps  = 1
| Regional cup first = [[2004 African Nations Cup|2004]]
| Regional cup best  = Group stage ([[2004 African Nations Cup|2004]])

| 2ndRegional cup apps = 4
| 2ndRegional name     = [[African Nations Championship]]
| 2ndRegional cup first= [[2011 African Nations Championship|2011]]
| 2ndRegional cup best = Quarter-finals ([[2016 African Nations Championship|2016]], [[2020 African Nations Championship|2020]])
}}

The '''Rwanda national football team''' represents [[Rwanda]] in international [[association football|football]] and is controlled by the [[Rwandese Association Football Federation]], the [[Sport governing body|governing body]] of [[football in Rwanda]], and competes as a member of the [[Confederation of African Football]] (CAF), as well as the [[CECAFA|Council for East and Central Africa Football Associations]] (CECAFA), a CAF sub-confederation that governs football in East and Central Africa. The team bears the nickname ''Amavubi'' ([[Kinyarwanda]] for ''The Wasps''), and primarily plays its home games at the [[Amahoro Stadium|Stade Amahoro]] in [[Kigali]], the nation's capital. They have never qualified for a [[FIFA World Cup|World Cup]] finals, and reached their only [[Africa Cup of Nations]] in [[2004 African Nations Cup|2004]].

==History==
Rwanda [[2004 African Cup of Nations qualification#Group 13|qualified]] for its first ever [[Africa Cup of Nations]] in the [[2004 African Cup of Nations|2004 edition]].<ref>{{cite news|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/football/africa/3438195.stm|title=Rwanda's football mercenaries|website=BBC Sport|publisher=BBC|date=28 January 2004|access-date=18 January 2013|archive-date=17 July 2013|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20130717041707/http://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/football/africa/3438195.stm|url-status=live}}</ref> At the tournament, they lost their opening match 2–1 to [[Tunisia national football team|Tunisia]] before winning their first ever point in the competition after a 1–1 draw against [[Guinea national football team|Guinea]].<ref>{{cite news|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport1/hi/football/africa/3433831.stm|title=Rwanda snatch dramatic point|website=BBC Sport|publisher=BBC|date=28 January 2004|access-date=18 January 2013|archive-date=17 July 2013|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20130717041702/http://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/football/africa/3433831.stm|url-status=live}}</ref> Rwanda went on to beat [[DR Congo national football team|DR Congo]] in their final group match by a 1–0 scoreline, but it wasn't enough, as elsewhere in the group, Guinea and Tunisia drew, meaning both teams progressed to the quarter-finals, and [[Rwanda]] were eliminated.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/football/africa/3448083.stm|title=Rwanda dumped out|website=BBC Sport|publisher=BBC|date=31 January 2004|access-date=18 January 2013|archive-date=10 February 2018|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180210004029/http://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/football/africa/3448083.stm|url-status=live}}</ref>

On 3 November 2007, [[Josip Kuže]] was appointed as head coach of Rwanda on a three-year deal,<ref>{{Cite news |date=November 3, 2007 |title=Kuze appointed Rwanda coach |url=http://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/football/africa/7076748.stm |access-date=2025-12-05 |work=BBC Sport |language=en-GB}}</ref> but left just two months later to manage Japanese club [[JEF United Chiba]], having managed just six matches.<ref>{{Cite news |date=January 9, 2008 |title=Croatian coach Kuze quits Rwanda |url=http://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/football/africa/7179126.stm |access-date=2025-12-05 |work=BBC Sport |language=en-GB}}</ref> [[Raoul Shungu]] was appointed as interim manager on 11 April 2008,<ref>{{Cite news |date=April 11, 2008 |title=Shungu takes charge of Rwanda |url=http://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/football/africa/7342908.stm |access-date=2025-12-05 |work=BBC Sport |language=en-GB}}</ref> with [[Branko Tucak]] appointed on a permanent basis just over two months later.<ref>{{Cite news |date=April 27, 2008 |title=Tucak appointed Rwanda coach |url=http://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/football/africa/7369926.stm |access-date=2025-12-05 |work=BBC Sport |language=en-GB}}</ref> After Rwanda finished bottom of their group in the [[2010 FIFA World Cup qualification – CAF third round|third round of 2010 FIFA World Cup qualifying]], Tucak was sacked in November 2009, with [[Eric Nshimiyimana]] appointed as his replacement on an interim basis.<ref>{{Cite news |date=November 24, 2009 |title=Rwanda sack Croatian coach Tucak |url=http://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/football/africa/8377171.stm |access-date=2025-12-05 |work=BBC Sport |language=en-GB}}</ref> In February 2010, Ghanaian [[Sellas Tetteh]] was appointed as the new national team manager.<ref>{{Cite news |date=February 18, 2010 |title=Rwanda seal Tetteh coaching deal |url=http://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/football/africa/8522862.stm |access-date=2025-12-05 |work=BBC Sport |language=en-GB}}</ref> Under Tetteh, Rwanda qualified for the [[2011 African Nations Championship]],<ref>{{Cite web |date=7 June 2010 |title=Sellas Tetteh qualifies Rwanda to CHAN tourney |url=https://www.myjoyonline.com/sellas-tetteh-qualifies-rwanda-to-chan-tourney/ |access-date=2025-12-05 |website=MyJoyOnline |language=en-US}}</ref> but finished bottom of their group after losing all three group stage matches.<ref>{{Cite web |title=African Nations Championship 2011 |url=https://www.rsssf.org/tables/2011anch.html |access-date=2025-12-05 |website=www.rsssf.org}}</ref> Tetteh resigned in September 2011.<ref>{{Cite news |date=September 6, 2011 |title=Ghanaian Sellas Tetteh steps down as coach of Rwanda |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/football/14809843 |access-date=2025-12-05 |work=BBC Sport |language=en-GB}}</ref>

[[Milutin Sredojević]] was appointed as Rwanda manager in November 2011,<ref>{{Cite news |date=November 1, 2011 |title=Milutin Sredojevic appointed Rwanda coach |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/football/15539635 |access-date=2025-12-05 |work=BBC Sport |language=en-GB}}</ref> though he was sacked in April 2013.<ref>{{Cite news |date=2013-04-17 |title=Rwanda part company with coach Milutin 'Micho' Sredojevic |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/football/22186908 |access-date=2025-12-05 |work=BBC Sport |language=en-GB}}</ref> Nshimiyimana was reappointed to succeed Sredojević,<ref>{{Cite web |last=Yahyaoui |first=Houssem |date=2013-04-22 |title=Rwanda: Eric Nshimiyimana, new coach! - Africa Top Sports |url=https://en.africatopsports.com/2013/04/22/rwanda-eric-nshimiyimana-new-coach/ |access-date=2025-12-05 |language=en-US}}</ref> though he was replaced by [[Stephen Constantine]] in May 2014.<ref>{{Cite news |date=2014-05-14 |title=Stephen Constantine appointed Rwanda coach |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/football/27374744 |access-date=2025-12-05 |work=BBC Sport |language=en-GB}}</ref> Under Constantine, Rwanda appeared to qualify for the group stage of qualifying for the [[2015 Africa Cup of Nations]], but were disqualified after fielding an ineligible player in a match against [[Congo national football team|Congo]].<ref>{{Cite news |last=Crocker |first=Sam |date=2014-10-09 |title=Stephen Constantine: I’m quite happy to do the jobs that nobody else wants |url=https://www.theguardian.com/football/2014/oct/09/stephen-constantine-rwanda-football |access-date=2025-12-05 |work=The Guardian |language=en-GB |issn=0261-3077}}</ref> Constantine resigned from his role in January 2015 however, in order to manage the [[India national football team|India national team]].<ref>{{Cite web |date=2015-01-15 |title=Constantine resigns from Amavubi |url=https://www.newtimes.co.rw/article/115101/Sports/constantine-resigns-from-amavubi |access-date=2025-12-05 |website=The New Times |language=en}}</ref>

[[Rwanda Football Federation]] technical director [[Lee Johnson (football coach)|Lee Johnson]] became interim manager before [[Johnathan McKinstry]] was appointed as Constantine's replacement in March 2015, with Johnson leaving his role as technical director to become Constantine's assistant manager in India shortly after.<ref>{{Cite web |date=2015-03-20 |title=Rwanda name 29-year-old McKinstry coach |url=https://www.espn.co.uk/football/story/_/id/37408308/rwanda-name-29-year-old-johnny-mckinstry-national-team-coach |access-date=2025-12-05 |website=ESPN.com |language=en}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |date=2015-04-14 |title=Lee Johnson resigns, to join Constantine in India |url=https://www.newtimes.co.rw/article/117909/Sports/lee-johnson-resigns-to-join-constantine-in-india |access-date=2025-12-06 |website=The New Times |language=en}}</ref> In his first competitive game in charge, McKinstry guided Rwanda to their first away win in four years with a 1–0 victory over Mozambique in the opening round of 2017 African Nations Cup qualifying.<ref>{{Cite news |last=Komugisha |first=Usher |author-link=Usher Komugisha |date=15 June 2015 |title=Rwanda/Mozambique: Country Ends Four-Year Away Jinx in Afcon Qualifiers |url=http://allafrica.com/stories/201506152015.html |access-date=16 May 2018 |work=The New Times (Kigali)}}</ref> In December 2015, Rwanda finished as runners-up in the [[2015 CECAFA Cup|CECAFA Senior Challenge Cup 2015]], having lost 1–0 to [[Uganda national football team|Uganda]] in the final.<ref name="auto3">{{Cite news |date=5 December 2015 |title=Uganda wins record 14th CECAFA title |url=http://www.newtimes.co.rw/section/article/2015-12-06/195035/ |access-date=16 May 2018 |work=The New Times {{!}} Rwanda |language=en}}</ref><ref name="auto4">{{Cite web |title=FERWAFA -Rwanda settle for second spot in Cecafa Challenge Cup |url=http://ferwafa.rw/spip.php?article605 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180517005311/http://ferwafa.rw/spip.php?article605 |archive-date=17 May 2018 |access-date=16 May 2018 |website=ferwafa.rw}}</ref> In January 2016, Rwanda reached the knock-out stages of a major competition for the first time in their history<ref>{{Cite news |date=23 January 2016 |title=Amavubi seek to top Group A unbeaten |url=http://www.newtimes.co.rw/section/article/2016-01-24/196385/ |access-date=16 May 2018 |work=The New Times {{!}} Rwanda |language=en}}</ref> after topping their group at the [[2016 African Nations Championship]].<ref>{{Cite news |title=Rwanda top Chan Group A |url=http://www.supersport.com/football/chan-2016/news/160121/Rwanda_top_Chan_Group_A |access-date=16 May 2018 |language=en-ZA}}</ref> Rwanda did however lose to eventual champions, [[DR Congo national football team|DR Congo]], 2–1 after extra time in the quarter-final.<ref>{{Cite news |date=2016 |title=CHAN 2016: DR Congo beat Rwanda 2–1 in extra-time |url=https://www.bbc.com/sport/football/35450007 |access-date=16 May 2018 |publisher=BBC Sport |language=en-GB}}</ref> In March 2016 McKinstry agreed a new two-year contract,<ref>{{Cite news |title=McKinstry extends Rwanda contract |url=http://www.supersport.com/football/africa/news/160323/McKinstry_extends_Rwanda_contract |access-date=16 May 2018 |language=en-ZA}}</ref> though he was sacked just five months later, with [[Jimmy Mulisa]] appointed on an interim basis.<ref>{{Cite news |date=2016-08-19 |title=Johnny Mckinstry 'surprised' after his sacking as Rwanda coach |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/football/37130404 |access-date=2025-12-05 |work=BBC Sport |language=en-GB}}</ref> The Rwanda Football Federation were later ordered by [[FIFA]] to compensate McKinstry $200,000 for unlawful dismissal, and though the Rwandan FA initially appealed this ruling, they later complied.<ref>{{Cite news |date=2018-05-08 |title=Rwanda files appeal to Fifa over McKinstry ruling |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/football/44041106 |access-date=2025-12-05 |work=BBC Sport |language=en-GB}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |last= |first= |date=2019-04-27 |title=McKinstry Welcomes Rwanda’s Move to Compensate Contract Breach |url=https://www.ktpress.rw/2019/04/mckinstry-welcomes-rwandas-move-to-compensate-contract-breach/ |access-date=2025-12-05 |website=KT PRESS |language=en-US}}</ref>

In November 2023, [[Torsten Spittler]] was appointed as head coach,<ref>{{Cite web |last=Abayisenga |first=Eddy |date=2023-11-01 |title=Torsten Spittler Appointed New Amavubi Coach |url=https://www.ktpress.rw/2023/11/torsten-spittler-appointed-new-amavubi-coach/ |access-date=2025-12-05 |website=KT PRESS |language=en}}</ref> though he was relieved of his duties in January 2025.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Gyimah |first=Edmund Okai |date=2025-01-23 |title=Spittler’s departure: How Amavubi may be affected |url=https://www.newtimes.co.rw/article/23475/sports/football/spittlers-departure-how-amavubi-may-be-affected |access-date=2025-12-05 |website=The New Times |language=en}}</ref> [[Adel Amrouche]] was appointed in his place in March 2025.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Kariuki |first=Erick |date=2025-03-03 |title=Adel Amrouche appointed Rwanda national team Head Coach |url=https://eastleighvoice.co.ke/sports/121389/adel-amrouche-appointed-rwanda-national-team-head-coach |access-date=2025-12-05 |website=The Eastleigh Voice News |language=en}}</ref>

==Team image==

===Kit===
In 2001, after adopting the new [[flag of Rwanda]], The Federation ''(FERWAFA)'' changed the color of the team kit. The new team kit consists of a yellow jersey, blue shorts and green socks for home matches, while their away kit is either  all white or all blue. [[Adidas]] has generally been the manufacturer for the Rwandan team since 2001.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.oldfootballshirts.com/en/teams/r/rwanda/old-rwanda-football-shirt-s26317.html|title=Rwanda football shirt 1994&nbsp;– 1996|work=oldfootballshirts.com|date=14 December 2011 |access-date=19 June 2016|archive-date=11 August 2016|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160811123522/http://www.oldfootballshirts.com/en/teams/r/rwanda/old-rwanda-football-shirt-s26317.html|url-status=live}}</ref> However, between 2004 and 2009, Rwanda used L-sport as their outfitter, and in 2015 the side started wearing kit provided by AMS, an emerging Australian supplier.

===Names===
Under the official [[List of FIFA country codes|FIFA Trigramme]] the team's name is abbreviated as '''RWA'''; this acronym is used by [[FIFA]], the [[Confederation of African Football|CAF]] and the [[Council for East and Central Africa Football Associations|CECAFA]] to identify the team in official competitions.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.fifa.com/associations/association=rwa/countryInfo.html|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20070706062335/http://www.fifa.com/associations/association=rwa/countryInfo.html|url-status=dead|archive-date=6 July 2007|title=Member Association&nbsp;– Rwanda|website=FIFA.com|publisher=FIFA|access-date=19 June 2016}}</ref> However the team was more commonly known as the '''RR''', the acronym for the country's official name, ''Repubulika y'u Rwanda'' or ''République du Rwanda'', which the local press used when they referred to the team as the RR XI. The national team is often referred to as ''Amavubi'' (The [[Wasp]]s).<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.goal.com/en/news/89/africa/2010/09/29/2142211/rwanda-football-boss-rallies-the-wasps-ahead-of-benin-clash|title=Rwanda football Boss Rallies The Wasps Ahead Of Benin Clash - Goal.com|date=29 September 2010|website=goal.com|publisher=Goal|access-date=19 June 2016|archive-date=26 December 2018|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20181226002554/https://www.goal.com/en/news/89/africa/2010/09/29/2142211/rwanda-football-boss-rallies-the-wasps-ahead-of-benin-clash%20|url-status=live}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://en.starafrica.com/football/rwandas-amavumbi-stars-invite-uganda-cranes-on-feb-6th.html|title=Rwanda's Amavumbi Stars invite Uganda Cranes on Feb 6th|date=2 February 2013|work=starafrica.com|access-date=19 June 2016|archive-date=4 March 2016|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160304040459/http://en.starafrica.com/football/rwandas-amavumbi-stars-invite-uganda-cranes-on-feb-6th.html|url-status=live}}</ref>

==Results and fixtures==

The following is a list of match results in the last 12 months, as well as any future matches that have been scheduled.

{{legend2|#CCFFCC|Win|border=1px solid #AAAAAA}}
{{legend2|#FFFFCC|Draw|border=1px solid #AAAAAA}}
{{legend2|#FFCCCC|Loss|border=1px solid #AAAAAA}}
{{legend2|#FFFFFF|Fixture|border=1px solid #AAAAAA}}

===2025===
{{Footballbox collapsible
|date       = 5 June
|time       = {{UTZ|17:00|1}}
|round      = [[Exhibition game|Friendly]]
|team1      = {{fb-rt|ALG}}
|score      = 2–0
|team2      = {{fb|RWA}}
|goals1     =
*[[Youcef Belaïli|Belaïli]] {{Goal|28}}
*[[Jaouen Hadjam|Hadjam]] {{Goal|58}}
|goals2     =
|stadium    = [[Mohamed Hamlaoui Stadium]]
|location   = [[Constantine, Algeria|Constantine]], Algeria
|attendance = 
|referee    = Mahmood Ali Ismail ([[Sudan Football Association|Sudan]])
|report     = https://int.soccerway.com/matches/2025/06/05/world/friendlies/algeria/rwanda/4642952/
|result     = l
}}
{{Footballbox collapsible
|date       = 9 June
|time       = {{UTZ|20:00|1}}
|round      = [[Exhibition game|Friendly]]
|team1      = {{fbl-rt|ALG|name=Algeria A'}}
|score      = 2–0
|team2      = {{fb|RWA}}
|goals1     =
*[[Adil Boulbina|Boulbina]] {{Goal|40}}
*[[Naoufel Khacef|Khacef]] {{Goal|71}}
|goals2     =
|stadium    = [[Mustapha Tchaker Stadium]]
|location   = [[Blida]], Algeria
|attendance = 
|referee    = 
|report     = 
|result     = l
}}
{{Football box collapsible
|round = [[2026 FIFA World Cup qualification – CAF Group C|2026 World Cup qualification]]
|date = 6 September
|time = {{UTZ|17:00|1}}
|team1 = {{fb-rt|NGA}}
|score = 1–0
|team2 = {{fb|RWA}}
|goals1 =
*[[Tolu Arokodare|Arokodare]] {{goal|51}}
|goals2 = 
|stadium = [[Godswill Akpabio International Stadium]]
|location = [[Uyo]], Nigeria
|attendance = 
|referee = Alhadi Allaou Mahamat ([[Chadian Football Federation|Chad]])
|report = https://www.fifa.com/en/match-centre/match/520/288282/288289/400020778
|result = L
}}
{{Football box collapsible
|round = [[2026 FIFA World Cup qualification – CAF Group C|2026 World Cup qualification]]
|date = 9 September
|time = {{UTZ|15:00|2}}
|team1 = {{fb-rt|ZIM}}
|score = 0–1
|team2 = {{fb|RWA}}
|goals1 =
|goals2 = 
*[[Gilbert Mugisha|Mugisha]] {{goal|40}}
|stadium = [[Orlando Stadium]]
|location = [[Johannesburg]], South Africa
|attendance = 
|referee = Abdou Abdel Mefire ([[Cameroonian Football Federation|Cameroon]])
|report = https://www.fifa.com/en/match-centre/match/520/288282/288289/400020780
|result = W
}}
{{Football box collapsible
|round = [[2026 FIFA World Cup qualification – CAF Group C|2026 World Cup qualification]]
|date = 10 October
|time = {{UTZ|18:00|2}}
|team1 = {{fb-rt|RWA}}
|score = 0–1
|team2 = {{fb|BEN}}
|goals1 =
|goals2 = 
*[[Tosin Aiyegun|Aiyegun]] {{goal|80}}
|stadium = [[Amahoro Stadium]]
|location = [[Kigali]], Rwanda
|attendance = 
|referee = Mohamed Maarouf Eid Mansour ([[Egyptian Football Association|Egypt]])
|report = https://www.fifa.com/en/match-centre/match/520/288282/288289/400021047
|result = L
}}
{{Football box collapsible
|round = [[2026 FIFA World Cup qualification – CAF Group C|2026 World Cup qualification]]
|date = 14 October
|time = {{UTZ|18:00|2}}
|team1 = {{fb-rt|RSA}}
|score = 3–0
|team2 = {{fb|RWA}}
|goals1 =
*[[Thalente Mbatha|Mbatha]] {{goal|5}}
*[[Oswin Appollis|Appollis]] {{goal|26}}
*[[Evidence Makgopa|Makgopa]] {{goal|72}}
|goals2 = 
|stadium = [[Mbombela Stadium]]
|location = [[Mbombela]], South Africa
|attendance = 
|referee = Mehrez Malki ([[Tunisian Football Federation|Tunisia]])
|report = https://www.fifa.com/en/match-centre/match/520/288282/288289/400021046
|result = L
}}

===2026===
{{football box collapsible
| round      = [[2026 FIFA Series (men's matches)|2026 FIFA Series]]
| date       = 27 March
| time       = {{UTZ|21:00|2}}
| team1      = {{fb-rt|RWA}}
| score      = 4–0
| team2      = {{fb|GRN}}
| report     = https://www.fifa.com/en/match-centre/match/10017/292887/292888/400021867?date=2026-03-28
| goals1     = 
*[[Leroy-Jacques Mickels|L. Mickels]] {{goal|45+2}}
*[[Jojea Kwizera|J. Kwizera]] {{goal|45+6}}
*[[Djihad Bizimana|Bizimana]] {{goal|69}}
*[[Hakim Sahabo|Sahabo]] {{goal|83}}
| goals2     = 
| location   = [[Kigali]], Rwanda
| stadium    = [[Amahoro Stadium]]
| attendance = 
| referee    = [[Haimanot Adane]] ([[Ethiopian Football Federation|Ethiopia]])
| result     = W
}}
{{football box collapsible
| round      = [[2026 FIFA Series (men's matches)|2026 FIFA Series]]
| date       = 30 March
| time       = {{UTZ|19:30|+2}}
| team1      = {{fb-rt|RWA}}
| score      = 2–0
| team2      = {{fb|EST}}
| report     = https://www.fifa.com/en/match-centre/match/10017/292887/292888/400021868
| goals1     = 
*[[Abeddy Biramahire|Biramahire]] {{goal|30}}
*[[Leroy-Jacques Mickels|L. Mickels]] {{goal|51}}
| goals2     = 
| location   = [[Kigali]], Rwanda
| stadium    = [[Amahoro Stadium]]
| attendance = 
| referee    = Ahmed Hassan Hussein ([[Somali Football Federation|Somalia]])
| result     = W
}}
{{Football box collapsible
| round      = [[Exhibition game#Association football|Friendly]]
| date       = 6 June
| time       =
| team1      = {{fb-rt|RWA}}
| score      = Cancelled
| report     =
| team2      = {{fb|COM}}
| goals1     = 
| goals2     = 
|stadium    = 
|location   = [[Marrakesh]], Morocco
| attendance = 
| referee    = 
| result     = v
}}
{{Football box collapsible
| round      = [[Exhibition game#Association football|Friendly]]
| date       = 8 June
| time       = {{UTZ|21:00|1}}
| team1      = {{fb-rt|TAN}}
| score      = Cancelled
| team2      = {{fb|RWA}}
| report     = 
| goals1     =
| goals2     =
| stadium    = 
| location   = 
| referee    = 
| result     = v
}}
{{footballbox collapsible
|format = 1
|round = [[2027 Africa Cup of Nations qualification|2027 AFCON qualification]]
|date = TBD
|time = TBD
|team1 = {{fb-rt|RWA}}
|score =
|team2 = {{fb|CPV}}
|report =
|goals1 =
|goals2 =
|location = TBD, Rwanda
|stadium = TBD
|attendance =
|referee =
|result =
}}
{{footballbox collapsible
|format = 1
|round = [[2027 Africa Cup of Nations qualification|2027 AFCON qualification]]
|date = TBD
|time = TBD
|team1 = {{fb-rt|CPV}}
|score =
|team2 = {{fb|RWA}}
|report =
|goals1 =
|goals2 =
|location = TBD, Cape Verde
|stadium = TBD
|attendance =
|referee =
|result =
}}
{{footballbox collapsible
|format = 1
|round = [[2027 Africa Cup of Nations qualification|2027 AFCON qualification]]
|date = TBD
|time = TBD
|team1 = {{fb-rt|MLI}}
|score =
|team2 = {{fb|RWA}}
|report =
|goals1 =
|goals2 =
|location = TBD, Mali
|stadium = TBD
|attendance =
|referee =
|result =
}}
{{footballbox collapsible
|format = 1
|round = [[2027 Africa Cup of Nations qualification|2027 AFCON qualification]]
|date = TBD
|time = TBD
|team1 = {{fb-rt|RWA}}
|score =
|team2 = {{fb|MLI}}
|report =
|goals1 =
|goals2 =
|location = TBD, Rwanda
|stadium = TBD
|attendance =
|referee =
|result =
}}
{{footballbox collapsible
|format = 1
|round = [[2027 Africa Cup of Nations qualification|2027 AFCON qualification]]
|date = TBD
|time = TBD
|team1 = {{fb-rt|RWA}}
|score =
|team2 = {{fb|LBR}}
|report =
|goals1 =
|goals2 =
|location = TBD, Rwanda
|stadium = TBD
|attendance =
|referee =
|result =
}}
{{footballbox collapsible
|format = 1
|round = [[2027 Africa Cup of Nations qualification|2027 AFCON qualification]]
|date = TBD
|time = TBD
|team1 = {{fb-rt|LBR}}
|score =
|team2 = {{fb|RWA}}
|report =
|goals1 =
|goals2 =
|location = TBD, Liberia
|stadium = TBD
|attendance =
|referee =
|result =
}}

==Coaching history==

[[File:Carlós Alós Ferrer (cropped) 2.jpg|thumb|200px|[[Carlos Alós]] was the manager of Rwanda between 2022 and 2023]]
:Caretaker managers are listed in ''italics''.
{{Div col|colwidth=22em}}
* {{flagicon|FRG}} [[Otto Pfister]] (1972–76)
* {{flagicon|TUR}} [[Metin Türel]] (1991)
* {{flagicon|RWA|1962}} [[Longin Rudasingwa]] (1998-1999)
* {{flagicon|GER}} [[Rudi Gutendorf]] (1999–00)
* {{flagicon|RWA|1962}} ''[[Longin Rudasingwa]]'' (2000–01)
* {{flagicon|SCG}} [[Ratomir Dujković]] (2001–04)
* {{flagicon|SWE}} [[Roger Palmgren]] (2004–05)
* {{flagicon|GER}} [[Michael Nees]] (2006–07)
* {{flagicon|CRO}} [[Josip Kuže]] (2007–08)
* {{flagicon|DRC}} ''[[Raoul Shungu]]'' (2008)
* {{flagicon|CRO}} [[Branko Tucak]] (2008–09)
* {{flagicon|RWA}} ''[[Eric Nshimiyimana]]'' (2009–10)
* {{flagicon|GHA}} [[Sellas Tetteh]] (2010–11)
* {{flagicon|SER}} [[Milutin Sredojević]] (2011–13)
* {{flagicon|RWA}} [[Eric Nshimiyimana]] (2013–14)
* {{flagicon|ENG}} [[Stephen Constantine]] (2014–2015)
* {{flagicon|ENG}} ''[[Lee Johnson (football coach)|Lee Johnson]]'' (2015)
* {{flagicon|NIR}} [[Johnny McKinstry]] (2015–16)
* {{flagicon|RWA}} ''[[Gilbert Kanyankore]]'' (2016)
* {{flagicon|RWA}} ''[[Jimmy Mulisa]]'' (2016)
* {{flagicon|GER}} [[Antoine Hey]]  (2017–2018)
* {{flagicon|RWA}} [[Vincent Mashami]] (2018–2022)
* {{flagicon|ESP}} [[Carlos Alós]] (2022–2023)
* {{flagicon|FRA}} ''[[Gerard Buscher]]'' (2023)
* {{flagicon|GER}} [[Torsten Spittler]] (2023–2025)
* {{flagicon|ALG}} [[Adel Amrouche]] (2025–2026)
* {{flagicon|ENG}} [[Stephen Constantine]] (2026–)
{{div col end}}

==Players==

===Current squad===
The following players were called up for the [[2026 FIFA Series (men's matches)]] matches against [[Grenada national football team|Grenada]] and either [[Kenya national football team|Kenya]] or [[Estonia national football team|Estonia]] on 27 and 30 March 2026.<ref>{{cite tweet |title=Urutonde rw’agateganyo rw’Abakinnyi bagize Ikipe y’Igihugu Amavubi bazatoranywamo abazahagararira u Rwanda muri|number=2033552572246593861|access-date=16 March 2026|user=FERWAFA |author-link=Rwanda Football Federation |date=16 March 2026}}</ref>

:''Caps and goals correct as of 5 June 2025, after the match against [[Algeria national football team|Algeria]].''

{{nat fs g start}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=GK|name=[[Fiacre Ntwari]]|age={{bda|1999|9|25|df=y}}|caps=21|goals=0|club=[[Kaizer Chiefs F.C.|Kaizer Chiefs]]|clubnat=RSA}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=GK|name=[[Adolphe Hakizimana]]|age={{bda|2002|2|5|df=y}}|caps=1|goals=0|club=[[APR F.C.|APR]]|clubnat=RWA}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=GK|name=[[Hugo Bigirimana]]|age={{birth date and age|2005|3|4|df=yes}}|caps=0|goals=0|club=[[FC La Chaux-de-Fonds|La Chaux-de-Fonds]]|clubnat=SUI}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=GK|name=[[Patience Niyongira]]|age={{birth date and age|2000|5|9|df=yes}}|caps=0|goals=0|club=[[Police F.C. (Rwanda)|Police]]|clubnat=RWA}}
{{nat fs break}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Emmanuel Imanishimwe]]|age={{Birth date and age|1995|2|2|df=y}}|caps=46|goals=0|club=[[AEL Limassol]]|clubnat=CYP}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Thierry Manzi]]|age={{Bda|1996|7|12|df=y}}|caps=40|goals=4|club=[[Al Ahli SC (Tripoli)|Al Ahli]]|clubnat=LBY}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Ange Mutsinzi]]|age={{Bda|1997|11|15|df=y}}|caps=30|goals=1|club=[[Zira FK|Zira]]|clubnat=AZE}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Claude Niyomugabo]]|age={{Bda|1998|8|2|df=y}}|caps=12|goals=0|club=[[APR F.C.|APR]]|clubnat=RWA}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Phanuel Kavita]]|age={{Bda|1993|3|9|df=yes}}|caps=1|goals=0|club=[[Birmingham Legion FC]]|clubnat=USA}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Gilbert Byiringiro]]|age={{Bda|2000|7|1|df=y}}|caps=0|goals=0|club=[[APR F.C.|APR]]|clubnat=RWA}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Yunus Nshimiyimana]]|age={{Bda|2001|12|31|df=y}}|caps=0|goals=0|club=[[APR F.C.|APR]]|clubnat=RWA}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Obed Uwumukiza]]|age=|caps=0|goals=0|club=[[Rayon Sports F.C.|Rayon Sports]]|clubnat=RWA}}
{{nat fs break}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Djihad Bizimana]]|age={{bda|1996|12|12|df=y}}|caps=47|goals=3|club=[[CS Constantine]]|clubnat=ALG}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Kevin Muhire]]|age={{Bda|1998|10|17|df=y}}|caps=38|goals=0|club=[[Jamus FC|Jamus]]|clubnat=SUD}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Bonheur Mugisha]]|age={{Bda|2000|1|1|df=y}}|caps=16|goals=0|club=[[Al Masry SC|Al Masry]]|clubnat=EGY}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Jean Bosco Ruboneka]]|age={{Bda|1999|1|1|df=y}}|caps=12|goals=0|club=[[APR F.C.|APR]]|clubnat=RWA}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Samuel Gueulette]]|age={{Bda|2000|5|19|df=y}}|caps=11|goals=0|club=[[RAAL La Louvière]]|clubnat=BEL}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Hakim Sahabo]]|age={{bda|2005|6|16|df=y}}|caps=9|goals=0|club=[[AEK Athens F.C.|AEK Athens]]|clubnat=GRE}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Sven Kalisa]]|age={{Bda|1997|3|14|df=y}}|caps=0|goals=0|club=[[FC Etzella Ettelbruck|Etzella Ettelbruck]]|clubnat=LUX}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[René Uwineza]]|age=|caps=0|goals=0|club=[[S.C. Kiyovu Sports|Kiyovu Sports]]|clubnat=RWA}}
{{nat fs break}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Innocent Nshuti]]|age={{bda|1998|1|31|df=y}}|caps=19|goals=4|club=[[Al-Wefaq Ajdabiya SC|Al-Wefaq Ajdabiya]]|clubnat=LBY}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Gilbert Mugisha]]|age={{Bda|1996|7|18|df=y}}|caps=19|goals=3|club=[[APR F.C.|APR]]|clubnat=RWA}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Abeddy Biramahire]]|age={{Bda|1998|10|04|df=yes}}|caps=12|goals=2|club=[[Assabah SC|Assabah]]|clubnat=LBY}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Lague Byiringiro]]|age={{Bda|2000|10|25|df=y}}|caps=13|goals=1|club=[[Police F.C. (Rwanda)|Police]]|clubnat=RWA}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Jojea Kwizera]]|age={{Bda|1999|1|1|df=y}}|caps=11|goals=2|club=[[Rhode Island FC]]|clubnat=USA}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Johan Kury]]|age={{Bda|2001|10|7|df=y}}|caps=0|goals=0|club=[[AC Bellinzona|Bellinzona]]|clubnat=SUI}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Leroy-Jacques Mickels]]|age={{Bda|1995|6|25|df=y}}|caps=0|goals=0|club=[[Zira FK|Zira]]|clubnat=AZE}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Joy-Lance Mickels]]|age={{Bda|1994|3|29|df=y}}|caps=0|goals=0|club=[[Sabah FK (Azerbaijan)|Sabah]]|clubnat=AZE}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Joy-Slayd Mickels]]|age={{Bda|1994|3|29|df=y}}|caps=0|goals=0|club=[[Karvan İK|Karvan]]|clubnat=AZE}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[David Niyo]]|age={{Bda|2007|6|23|df=y}}|caps=0|goals=0|club=[[NK Veres Rivne|Veres Rivne]]|clubnat=UKR}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Karl Matteo Ndayishimiye]]|age=|caps=0|goals=0|club=[[KVC Wilrijk|Wilrijk]]|clubnat=BEL}}
{{nat fs end}}

===Recent call-ups===
The following players have been called up for Rwanda in the last 12 months.

{{nat fs r start}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=GK|name=Pierre Ishimwe|age={{birth date and age|2002|06|16|df=yes}}|caps=3|goals=0|club=[[APR F.C.|APR]]|clubnat=RWA|latest=v. {{fb|RSA}}, 14 October 2025}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=GK|name=[[Clement Twizere Buhake]]|age={{birth date and age|1996|07|09|df=yes}}|caps=2|goals=0|club=[[Ullensaker/Kisa IL|Ullensaker/Kisa]]|clubnat=NOR|latest=v. {{fb|RSA}}, 14 October 2025}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=GK|name=[[Maxime Wenssens]]|age={{bda|2001|11|17|df=y}}|caps=1|goals=0|club=[[K.V.V. Thes Sport Tessenderlo|Thes Sport]]| clubnat=BEL|latest=v. {{fb|LES}}, 25 March 2025}}
{{nat fs break}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Aly-Enzo Hamon]]|age={{birth date and age|2003|03|30|df=yes}}|caps=1|goals=0|club=[[Angoulême Charente FC|Angoulême Charente]]|clubnat=FRA|latest=v. {{fb|RSA}}, 14 October 2025}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=DF|name=Darryl Nkulikiyimana|age={{birth date and age|2005|05|24|df=yes}}|caps=0|goals=0|club=[[Standard Liège]]|clubnat=BEL|latest=v. {{fb|RSA}}, 14 October 2025}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=DF|name=Emmanuel Nshimiyimana|age=|caps=0|goals=0|club=[[Rayon Sports F.C.|Rayon Sports]]|clubnat=RWA|latest=v. {{fb|RSA}}, 14 October 2025}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Fitina Omborenga]]|age={{Bda|1996|5|20|df=y}}|caps=60|goals=1|club=[[APR F.C.|APR]]|clubnat=RWA|latest=v. {{fb|ZIM}}, 9 September 2025}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=DF|name=Alexis Nduwayo|age=|caps=0|goals=0|club=[[APR F.C.|APR]]|clubnat=RWA|latest=v. {{fb|ZIM}}, 9 September 2025}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Clement Niyigena]]|age={{Bda|2001|2|17|df=y}}|caps=5|goals=0|club=[[APR F.C.|APR]]|clubnat=RWA|latest=v. {{fb|LES}}, 25 March 2025}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=DF|name=Hakim Bugingo|age={{birth date and age|2001|01|01|df=yes}}|caps=0|goals=0|club=[[Rayon Sports F.C.|Rayon Sports]]|clubnat=RWA|latest=v. {{fb|LES}}, 25 March 2025}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Aimable Nsabimana]]|age={{Bda|1997|6|6|df=y}}|caps=8|goals=0|club=[[S.C. Kiyovu Sports|Kiyovu Sports]]|clubnat=RWA|latest=v. {{fb|NGA}}, 10 September 2024}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=DF|name=Christian Ishimwe|age={{Bda|1999|2|1|df=y}}|caps=4|goals=0|club=[[APR F.C.|APR]]|clubnat=RWA|latest=v. {{fb|NGA}}, 10 September 2024}}
{{nat fs break}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Bryan Ngwabije]]|age={{birth date and age|1998|05|30|df=yes}}|caps=3|goals=0|club=[[FC Dieppe|Dieppe]]|clubnat=FRA|latest=v. {{fb|RSA}}, 14 October 2025}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=MF|name=Dylan Maes|age={{Bda|2001|3|7|df=y}}|caps=0|goals=0|club=''Free agent''|clubnat=|latest=v. {{fb|ZIM}}, 9 September 2025}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=MF|name=Claude Kayibanda|age={{birth date and age|2006|05|28|df=yes}}|caps=0|goals=0|club=[[Bedford Town F.C.|Bedford Town]]|clubnat=ENG|latest=v. {{fb|NGA}}, 6 September 2025}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Djabel Manishimwe]]|age={{birth date and age|1998|05|10|df=yes}}|caps=15|goals=0|club=[[Naft Al-Wasat SC|Naft Al-Wasat]]|clubnat=IRQ|latest=v. {{fb|LES}}, 25 March 2025}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Steve Rubanguka]]|age={{Bda|1996|10|14|df=y}}|caps=12|goals=0|club=[[Al-Nojoom FC|Al-Nojoom]]|clubnat=KSA|latest=v. {{fb|LES}}, 25 March 2025}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[York Rafael]]|age={{Birth date and age|1999|3|17|df=y}}|caps=10|goals=0|club=[[ZED FC|ZED]]|clubnat=EGY|latest=v. {{fb|LES}}, 25 March 2025}}
{{nat fs break}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=FW|name=Arthur Gitego|age={{Bda|2002|1|1|df=y}}|caps=5|goals=0|club=[[FUS Rabat]]|clubnat=MAR|latest=v. {{fb|RSA}}, 14 October 2025}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=FW|name=Anicet Ishimwe|age={{birth date and age|2003|04|06|df=yes}}|caps=0|goals=0|club=[[Olympique Béja]]|clubnat=TUN|latest=v. {{fb|RSA}}, 14 October 2025}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=FW|name=Didier Mugisha|age={{bda|2001|1|26|df=y}}|caps=3|goals=0|club=[[Police F.C. (Rwanda)|Police]]|clubnat=RWA|latest=v. {{fb|LES}}, 25 March 2025}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=FW|name=Hadji Iraguha|age={{Bda|1998|7|18|df=y}}|caps=1|goals=0|club=[[Rayon Sports F.C.|Rayon Sports]]|clubnat=RWA|latest=v. {{fb|LES}}, 25 March 2025}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=FW|name=Yves Habimana|age=|caps=0|goals=0|club=[[Free agent]]|latest=v. {{fb|LES}}, 25 March 2025}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=FW|name=Olivier Dushimimana|age={{Bda|2000|4|10|df=y}}|caps=0|goals=0|club=[[APR F.C.|APR]]|clubnat=RWA|latest=v. {{fb|NGA}}, 10 September 2024}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Patrick Sibomana]]|age={{Birth date and age|1996|10|15|df=y}}|caps=19|goals=2|club=[[Gor Mahia F.C.|Gor Mahia]]|clubnat=KEN|latest=v. {{fb|LES}}; 11 June 2024}}
{{nat fs break}}
*<sup>DEC</sup> Player refused to join the team after the call-up.
*<sup>INJ</sup> Player withdrew from the squad due to an injury.
*<sup>PRE</sup> Preliminary squad.
*<sup>RET</sup> Player has retired from international football.
*<sup>SUS</sup> Serving suspension.
{{nat fs end}}

==Player records==
{{Updated|14 October 2025}}<ref>{{cite web |title=Rwanda |url=https://www.national-football-teams.com/country/153/Rwanda.html |website=National Football Teams}}</ref>
:''Players in '''bold''' are still active with Rwanda.''

===Most appearances===

[[File:Niyonzima haruna.jpg|200px|right|thumb|[[Haruna Niyonzima]] is Rwanda's most capped player with 112 appearances.]]

{| class="wikitable sortable" style="text-align: center;"
|-
!width=30px|Rank
!class="unsortable" style="width:175px;"|Name
!width=50px|Caps
!width=50px|Goals
!class="unsortable" style="width:100px;"|Career
|-
|1
|align=left|[[Haruna Niyonzima]]
|112
|6
|2006–2022
|-
|2
|align=left|[[Jean-Baptiste Mugiraneza]]
|89
|6
|2006–2018
|-
|3
|align=left|'''[[Fitina Omborenga]]'''
|79
|1
|2013–present
|-
|4
|align=left|[[Jean-Claude Iranzi]]
|76
|3
|2008–2019
|-
|rowspan=2|5
|align=left|'''[[Djihad Bizimana]]'''
|70
|3
|2015–present
|-
|align=left|[[Olivier Karekezi]]
|70
|24
|2000–2013
|-
|7
|align=left|[[Jean-Luc Ndayishimiye]]
|64
|0
|2007–2019
|-
|8
|align=left|'''[[Meddie Kagere]]'''
|60
|15
|2011–present
|-
|9
|align=left|[[Jacques Tuyisenge]]
|59
|16
|2011–2022
|-
|10
|align=left|'''[[Thierry Manzi]]'''
|57
|4
|2016–present
|-
|}

===Top goalscorers===

[[File:OlivierKarekezi 110723GIFS-Öster 2-1 6892.jpg|200px|right|thumb|[[Olivier Karekezi]] is Rwanda's top scorer with 24 goals.]]

{|class="wikitable sortable" style="text-align: center;"
|-
!width=30px|Rank
!class="unsortable" width=175px|Name
!width=50px|Goals
!width=50px|Caps
!width=50px|Ratio
!class="unsortable" width=100px|Career
|-
|1
|align="left"|[[Olivier Karekezi]]
|24
|70
|{{#expr:24/70 round 2}}
|2000–2013
|-
|2
|align="left"|[[Jacques Tuyisenge]]
|16
|59
|{{#expr:16/59 round 2}}
|2011–2022
|-
|3
|align="left"|'''[[Meddie Kagere]]'''
|15
|60
|{{#expr:15/60 round 2}}
|2011–present
|-
|4
|align="left"|[[Jean Lomami]]
|14
|25
|{{#expr:14/25 round 2}}
|2003–2009
|-
|5
|align="left"|[[Labama Bokota]]
|13
|33
|{{#expr:13/33 round 2}}
|2007–2012
|-
|6
|align="left"|[[Ernest Sugira]]
|12
|36
|{{#expr:12/36 round 2}}
|2015–2022
|-
|7
|align="left"|[[Saïd Makasi]]
|9
|26
|{{#expr:9/26 round 2}}
|2003–2009
|-
|8
|align="left"|[[Jimmy Gatete|Jimmy Gatété]]
|8
|41
|{{#expr:8/41 round 2}}
|1996–2009
|-
|rowspan=2|9
|align="left"|[[Daddy Birori]]
|7
|25
|{{#expr:7/25 round 2}}
|2009–2014
|-
|align="left"|'''[[Muhadjiri Hakizimana]]'''
|7
|35
|{{#expr:7/35 round 2}}
|2016–present
|-
|}

==Competition records==
===FIFA World Cup===
{| class="wikitable" style="text-align: center;"
!colspan=9|[[FIFA World Cup]] record
!width=1% rowspan=39|
!colspan=6|[[FIFA World Cup qualification|Qualification]] record
|-
!Year
!Round
!Position
!{{Tooltip|Pld|Games played}}
!{{Tooltip|W|Won}}
!{{Tooltip|D|Drawn}}*
!{{Tooltip|L|Lost}}
!{{Tooltip|GF|Goals for}}
!{{Tooltip|GA|Goals against}}
!{{Tooltip|Pld|Games played}}
!{{Tooltip|W|Won}}
!{{Tooltip|D|Drawn}}
!{{Tooltip|L|Lost}}
!{{Tooltip|GF|Goals for}}
!{{Tooltip|GA|Goals against}}
|-
| {{flagicon|Argentina}} [[1978 FIFA World Cup|1978]]
|colspan=8 |''Not a FIFA member''
|colspan=6 |''Not a FIFA member''
|-
| {{flagicon|Spain}} [[1982 FIFA World Cup|1982]]
|colspan=8 rowspan=2|''Did not enter''
|colspan=6 rowspan=2|''Declined participation''
|-
|{{flagicon|Mexico}} [[1986 FIFA World Cup|1986]]
|-
| {{flagicon|Italy}} [[1990 FIFA World Cup|1990]]
|colspan=8 |''Withdrew''
|colspan=6 |''Withdrew''
|-
| {{flagicon|United States}} [[1994 FIFA World Cup|1994]]
|colspan=8 |''Did not enter''
|colspan=6 |''Declined participation''
|-
| {{flagicon|France|1974}} [[1998 FIFA World Cup|1998]]
|colspan=8 rowspan=8 |''Did not qualify''
| 2
| 0
| 0
| 2
| 1
| 5
|-
| {{flagicon|South Korea|1997}} {{flagicon|Japan}} [[2002 FIFA World Cup|2002]]
| 2
| 0
| 1
| 1
| 2
| 4
|-
| {{flagicon|Germany}} [[2006 FIFA World Cup|2006]]
| 12
| 2
| 3
| 7
| 10
| 17
|-
| {{flagicon|South Africa}} [[2010 FIFA World Cup|2010]]
| 10
| 3
| 2
| 5
| 8
| 11
|-
| {{flagicon|Brazil}} [[2014 FIFA World Cup|2014]]
| 8
| 1
| 3
| 4
| 7
| 13
|-
| {{flagicon|Russia}} [[2018 FIFA World Cup|2018]]
| 2
| 0
| 0	
| 2
| 1
| 4
|-
|{{flagicon|Qatar}} [[2022 FIFA World Cup|2022]]
|8||2||1||5||12||9
|-
|{{flagicon|Canada}} {{flagicon|Mexico}} {{flagicon|United States}} [[2026 FIFA World Cup|2026]] 
|10||3||2||5||5||9
|-
|{{flagicon|Morocco}} {{flagicon|Portugal}} {{flagicon|Spain}} [[2030 FIFA World Cup|2030]]
|colspan=8 rowspan=2 | ''To be determined''
|-
|{{flagicon|Saudi Arabia}} [[2034 FIFA World Cup|2034]] 
|-
!Total
! 
!{{Tooltip|0/11|Number of tournaments qualified for}}
! 
! 
! 
! 
!
!
!54
!11
!12
!31
!46
!72
|}

{{col-begin}}
{{col-2}}

===Africa Cup of Nations===
{{main|Rwanda at the Africa Cup of Nations}}
{| class="wikitable" style="text-align: center;"
|-
!colspan=9|[[Africa Cup of Nations]] record
|-
!colspan=9|Appearances: 1
|-
!Year
!Round
!Position
!{{Tooltip|Pld|Games played}}
!{{Tooltip|W|Won}}
!{{Tooltip|D|Drawn}}
!{{Tooltip|L|Lost}}
!{{Tooltip|GF|Goals for}}
!{{Tooltip|GA|Goals against}}
|-
|{{flagicon|Sudan|1956}} [[1957 Africa Cup of Nations|1957]]||colspan=8 rowspan=3|''Part of {{fb|Belgium}}''
|-
|{{flagicon|UAR}} [[1959 Africa Cup of Nations|1959]]
|-
|{{flagicon|ETH|1897}} [[1962 Africa Cup of Nations|1962]]
|-
|{{flagicon|GHA}} [[1963 Africa Cup of Nations|1963]]||colspan=8 rowspan=7|''Not affiliated to CAF''
|-
|{{flagicon|TUN|1959}} [[1965 Africa Cup of Nations|1965]]
|-
|{{flagicon|ETH|1897}} [[1968 Africa Cup of Nations|1968]]
|-
|{{flagicon|SDN|1956}} [[1970 Africa Cup of Nations|1970]]
|-
|{{flagicon|CMR|1961}} [[1972 Africa Cup of Nations|1972]]
|-
|{{flagicon|EGY|1972}} [[1974 Africa Cup of Nations|1974]]
|-
|{{flagicon|ETH|1975}} [[1976 Africa Cup of Nations|1976]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Ghana}} [[1978 African Cup of Nations|1978]]||colspan=8 rowspan=2|''Did not enter''
|-
|{{flagicon|Nigeria}} [[1980 African Cup of Nations|1980]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Libya|1977}} [[1982 African Cup of Nations|1982]]||colspan=8 rowspan=2|''Did not qualify''
|-
|{{flagicon|Ivory Coast}} [[1984 African Cup of Nations|1984]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Egypt}} [[1986 African Cup of Nations|1986]]
|colspan=8|''Did not enter''
|-
|{{flagicon|Morocco}} [[1988 African Cup of Nations|1988]]
|colspan=8|''Withdrew''
|-
|{{flagicon|Algeria}} [[1990 African Cup of Nations|1990]]||colspan=8 rowspan=5|''Did not enter''
|-
|{{flagicon|Senegal}} [[1992 African Cup of Nations|1992]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Tunisia}} [[1994 African Cup of Nations|1994]]
|-
|{{flagicon|South Africa}} [[1996 African Cup of Nations|1996]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Burkina Faso}} [[1998 African Cup of Nations|1998]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Ghana}} {{flagicon|Nigeria}} [[2000 African Cup of Nations|2000]]||colspan=8 rowspan=2|''Did not qualify''
|-
|{{flagicon|Mali}} [[2002 African Cup of Nations|2002]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Tunisia}} [[2004 African Cup of Nations|2004]]
|Group stage
|9th
|3
|1
|1
|1
|3
|3
|-
|{{flagicon|Egypt}} [[2006 Africa Cup of Nations|2006]]||colspan=8 rowspan=5|''Did not qualify''
|-
|{{flagicon|Ghana}} [[2008 Africa Cup of Nations|2008]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Angola}} [[2010 Africa Cup of Nations|2010]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Equatorial Guinea}} {{flagicon|Gabon}} [[2012 Africa Cup of Nations|2012]]
|-
|{{flagicon|South Africa}} [[2013 Africa Cup of Nations|2013]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Equatorial Guinea}} [[2015 Africa Cup of Nations|2015]]
|colspan=8|''Disqualified''
|-
|{{flagicon|Gabon}} [[2017 Africa Cup of Nations|2017]]||colspan=8 rowspan=5|''Did not qualify''
|-
|{{flagicon|Egypt}} [[2019 Africa Cup of Nations|2019]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Cameroon}} [[2021 Africa Cup of Nations|2021]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Ivory Coast}} [[2023 Africa Cup of Nations|2023]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Morocco}} [[2025 Africa Cup of Nations|2025]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Kenya}} {{flagicon|Tanzania}} {{flagicon|Uganda}} [[2027 Africa Cup of Nations|2027]]
|rowspan=2 colspan=8|''To be determined''
|-
|{{flagicon|}} [[2029 Africa Cup of Nations|2029]]
|-
!Total
!Group stage
!1/35
!3
!1
!1
!1
!3
!3
|}
{{col-2}}

===African Nations Championship===
{| class="wikitable" style="text-align: center;"
!colspan=9|[[African Nations Championship]] record
|-
!colspan=9|Appearances: 4
|-
!Year
!Round
!Position
!{{Tooltip|Pld|Games played}}
!{{Tooltip|W|Won}}
!{{Tooltip|D|Drawn}}
!{{Tooltip|L|Lost}}
!{{Tooltip|GF|Goals for}}
!{{Tooltip|GA|Goals against}}
|-
|{{flagicon|Cote d'Ivoire}} [[2009 African Nations Championship|2009]]||Colspan=8|''Did not qualify''
|- 
|{{flagicon|Sudan}} [[2011 African Nations Championship|2011]]||Group stage||16th||3||0||0||3||2||7
|-  
|{{flagicon|South Africa}} [[2014 African Nations Championship|2014]]||Colspan=8|''Did not qualify''
|-
|style="border: 3px solid red"|{{flagicon|Rwanda}} [[2016 African Nations Championship|2016]] ||Quarter-finals||5th||4||2||0||2||5||7
|- 
|{{flagicon|Morocco}} [[2018 African Nations Championship|2018]]||Group stage||13th ||3||1||1||1||1||1
|-
|{{flagicon|Cameroon}} [[2020 African Nations Championship|2020]]|||Quarter-finals||7th||4||1||2||1||3||3
|-
|{{flagicon|Algeria}} [[2022 African Nations Championship|2022]]||Colspan=8 rowspan=2|''Did not qualify''
|-
||{{flagicon|Kenya}} {{flagicon|Tanzania}} {{flagicon|Uganda}} [[2024 African Nations Championship|2024]]
|-
!Total||Quarter-finals||4/8||14||4||3||7||11||18
|}
{{col-end}}

===CECAFA Cup===
{{Main|CECAFA Cup}}

==Honours==
===Regional===
* '''[[CECAFA Cup]]'''
** {{Gold1}} '''Champions (1):''' [[1999 CECAFA Cup|1999]]
** {{Silver2}} Runners-up (6): [[2003 CECAFA Cup|2003]], [[2005 CECAFA Cup|2005]], [[2007 CECAFA Cup|2007]], [[2009 CECAFA Cup|2009]], [[2011 CECAFA Cup|2011]], [[2015 CECAFA Cup|2015]]
** {{Bronze3}} Third place (4): [[1999 CECAFA Cup|1999]], [[2001 CECAFA Cup|2001]], [[2002 CECAFA Cup|2002]], [[2006 CECAFA Cup|2006]]
'''Friendly'''
* '''[[FIFA Series]]'''
**{{gold1}} '''Champions (1):''' [[2026 FIFA Series (men's matches)#Rwanda|2026 Rwanda]]<ref name="2026 FIFA Series result">{{cite web |author=<!-- not stated --> |date=March 30, 2026 |title=New Zealand net four as quartet win shootouts |url=https://www.fifa.com/en/tournaments/series/articles/fifa-series-matchday-six-round-up |website=fifa.com |location= |publisher=[[FIFA]] |access-date=April 6, 2026}}</ref>

==See also==
* [[Rwanda national under-17 football team]]

==References==
{{Reflist}}

==External links==
{{Commons category}}
<!-- per [[WP:ELMINOFFICIAL]], choose one official website only -->
* {{official website|http://www.ferwafa.rw}}
* [https://www.cafonline.com/member-associations/rwa/ Rwanda] at [[Confederation of African Football|CAF]]
* [https://www.fifa.com/about-fifa/associations/RWA Rwanda] at [[FIFA]]

{{Navboxes|
|title=Rwanda squad
|bg=#00A1DE
|fg=#FAD201
|bordercolor=#20603D
|list1=
{{Rwanda Squad 2004 African Cup of Nations}}
{{Rwanda national football team managers}}
}}
{{Football in Rwanda}}
{{CECAFA}}
{{CAF teams}}
{{National sports teams of Rwanda}}

{{DEFAULTSORT:Rwanda National Football Team}}
[[Category:Rwanda national football team| ]]
[[Category:African national association football teams]]
